codeReview.summary: ConfigGet and ConfigSet commands are correctly implemented in the daemon. The implementation writes only to settings.local.json (not settings.json), supports dot notation for nested keys, and returns the source file. Tests use #[serial] to prevent race conditions with cwd changes.

issues.description: handle_command function comment does not list ConfigGet, ConfigSet, MemoryQuery, or CostQuery as supported commands - the comment is stale

CLI only handles ConfigValue display but does not have a 'swell config get' or 'swell config set' command for users to invoke ConfigGet/ConfigSet. The REPL's /config is a placeholder. This appears intentional since the feature is 'daemon protocol' commands, not CLI commands.

sharedStateObservations.description: Test coverage does not include an end-to-end test that does ConfigSet followed by ConfigGet to verify the override is read back. The test 'test_config_set_writes_only_to_settings_local_json' verifies the file I/O but doesn't then call ConfigGet to confirm the ConfigLoader returns the override.

summary: Feature passes review. The daemon-side implementation is correct: ConfigGet reads from ConfigLoader and returns value with source file; ConfigSet writes atomically to settings.local.json only. CLI handling exists for displaying ConfigValue responses. The primary gap is that no CLI command (swell config get/set) exists to invoke these daemon commands, but this appears intentional since the feature description emphasizes 'daemon protocol' commands rather than CLI commands. Test coverage is comprehensive for unit-level behavior but doesn't test ConfigGet reading back a ConfigSet override in a single test.
